# Distinguished Engineer AI
[...]
Posted May 25, 2026
[...]
At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience. Delta is investing in and growing our capabilities across our IT global technology hubs, to continually improve the end-to-end travel experience for our customers and enable our 90,000 Delta people who serve them.
[...]
- We are seeking a Distinguished Engineer to serve as a senior technical authority and strategic leader in designing and scaling next-generation cloud and Agentic AI platforms. This role combines deep expertise in AWS cloud architecture and developing and democratizing building Agentic applications. The Distinguished Engineer will drive technical vision, influence architecture across teams, and lead the development of highly scalable, secure, and resilient systems that power mission-critical applications. This role requires a hands-on technical leader who thrives in solving complex distributed systems challenges and shaping long-term platform strategy. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
[...]
- Define and drive the technical vision and architecture for cloud-native and AI-enabled platforms across the organization
[...]
- Lead the design and implementation of large-scale distributed systems using AWS services and microservices
[...]
- Architect and optimize Agentic applications
[...]
- Define and evangelize Delta’s Agentic architecture and establish the taxonomy of Delta AI Agents
[...]
- Establish engineering best practices for scalability, reliability, performance, and security
[...]
- Collaborate with product, data science, and infrastructure teams to align technical strategy with business goals
[...]
- Evaluate emerging technologies and incorporate innovative solutions into the platform roadmap
[...]
- Lead architectural reviews and guide complex technical decision-making
[...]
- Champion DevOps and automation practices, including CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure as code, even for building agentic applications
[...]
- WHAT YOU NEED TO SUCCEED (MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS)
[...]
- 15+ years of software engineering experience with a strong focus on large-scale distributed systems
- Hands-on experience with AI driven development tools
- Hands-on experience designing and developing Agentic applications
[...]
- Deep expertise in AWS cloud architecture (e.g., EC2, Lambda, ECS/EKS, S3, RDS/DynamoDB, API Gateway)
[...]
- Advanced proficiency modern backend frameworks (e.g., Spring Boot, Quarkus)
[...]
- Strong experience designing and deploying AI/ML solutions in production environments
[...]
- Proven track record of leading architecture for high-availability, cloud-native systems
[...]
- Experience with microservices architecture, containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), and event-driven systems
[...]
- Strong understanding of data architecture, APIs, and system integration
[...]
- WHAT WILL GIVE YOU A COMPETITIVE EDGE (PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS)

- Bachelors Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems or related technical field is preferred
- Experience working in an airline technology environment is preferred
- Experience with large-scale AI platforms or generative AI systems

results.title: Senior Data Engineer - Event-Driven Data Pipelines on AWS at Delta Airlines - Work from Home Opportunity with Competitive Pay
highlights:
Senior Data Engineer - Event-Driven Data Pipelines on AWS at Delta Airlines - Work from Home Opportunity with Competitive Pay
[...]
# Senior Data Engineer - Event-Driven Data Pipelines on AWS at Delta Airlines - Work from Home Opportunity with Competitive Pay
[...]
## Unlock a Rewarding Career with Delta Airlines: Join Our Team as a Senior Data Engineer
[...]
Delta Airlines, a renowned leader in the aviation industry, is seeking a highly skilled and experienced Senior Data Engineer to join our team. As a Senior Data Engineer, you will play a crucial role in designing, building, and maintaining our event-driven data pipelines on Amazon Web Services (AWS). If you have a passion for data engineering, a strong background in AWS native event-driven services, and a commitment to excellence, we invite you to explore this exciting opportunity.
[...]
At Delta Airlines, we are dedicated to connecting people and communities around the world. Our culture is rooted in a shared commitment to living our values - Care, Honesty, Strength, Employee Service, and Collaboration - every day, in everything we do. As a Senior Data Engineer, you will be an integral part of our team, working closely with stakeholders to understand and define data requirements, implementing data quality checks and monitoring, and optimizing data pipelines for performance and cost.
[...]
## Key Responsibilities
[...]
- Design, build, and maintain event-driven data pipelines on AWS, leveraging services such as Amazon EventBridge, AWS Lambda, and AWS Step Functions.
[...]
- Collaborate with stakeholders to understand and define data requirements, ensuring that our data pipelines meet the needs of our business.
[...]
- Implement data quality checks and monitoring to ensure the integrity and accuracy of our data.
[...]
- Optimize data pipelines for performance and cost, staying up-to-date on the latest AWS advancements and best practices.
[...]
- Develop and implement personalization algorithms using Python and Pydantic models, driving business growth and customer engagement.
[...]
- Build and maintain DynamoDB tables and NoSQL databases, ensuring data is properly stored and retrieved.
[...]
- Utilize Amazon Kinesis to collect, process, and analyze streaming data, providing real-time insights to our business.
[...]
- A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Science, or a related field.
- A strong commitment to safety and security, consistently prioritizing the well-being of yourself, others, and personal data.
- An embracing attitude towards diverse people, thinking, and styles.
[...]
## Preferred Qualifications
[...]
To give you a competitive edge, we prefer candidates with:
[...]
- A four-year degree in Computer Science, Information Science, or a related field.
- 3+ years of experience in data engineering, with a strong background in AWS native event-driven services.
- Experience with data modeling, data quality, and data security, ensuring that our data pipelines are robust and reliable.
[...]
- Proficiency in Python and serverless technologies, with experience developing personalization algorithms using Pydantic models.
- Excellent problem-solving and debugging skills, with the ability to work independently and as part of a team.
[...]
- Experience with Amazon Kinesis, DynamoDB, and NoSQL databases, providing a strong foundation in data storage and retrieval.
[...]
At Delta Airlines, we are committed to supporting the growth and well-being of our employees. As a Senior Data Engineer, you will have access to a comprehensive benefits package, including:
[...]
If you are a motivated and experienced Senior Data Engineer looking for a new challenge, we invite you to join our team at Delta Airlines. With a competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and opportunities for career growth and development, this is an exciting opportunity to take your career to the next level. Apply now and discover a rewarding career with a world-renowned airline.

highlights:
AI Software Engineer – Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning Solutions Developer | Hirevector
[...]
# AI Software Engineer – Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning Solutions Developer
[...]
### The Role: AI Software Engineer
[...]
Are you ready to join a team that's reshaping the future of aviation through artificial intelligence? We are currently seeking a talented and motivated AI Software Engineer to become an integral part of our Innovation team. In this role, you'll have the unique opportunity to work on initiatives that span the entire enterprise, solving real-world, large-scale problems using state-of-the-art AI technologies.
[...]
This is more than just a coding position—it's a chance to be at the forefront of technological innovation in the travel industry. You'll collaborate with experts across disciplines, partner with specialized business units, and help democratize AI throughout our organization. Your work will directly impact how millions of travelers experience air travel, making their journeys smoother, more efficient, and more enjoyable.
[...]
As an AI Software Engineer at arenaflex, you will play a pivotal role in identifying, defining, and solving business challenges through artificial intelligence. Your responsibilities will include:
[...]
Work collaboratively with cross-functional teams and partner departments to identify and define business problems that are suitable for AI-driven solutions. You'll serve as the bridge between technical capabilities and business needs.
[...]
Actively participate in developing the strategy surrounding which frameworks, technologies, and infrastructure our team should utilize for our proof-of-concepts and pilots for AI solutions. You'll help shape the technical direction of our innovation efforts.
[...]
Build verification of concepts and prototypes that can be used to demonstrate the impact of artificial intelligence across Delta's various specialty units. You'll transform theoretical ideas into tangible demonstrations.
[...]
Construct a suite of reusable models and solutions that enable rapid prototyping and accelerate the deployment of AI capabilities throughout the organization.
[...]
Foster an AI-first mindset and help democratize artificial intelligence for arenaflex, ensuring that teams across the organization can leverage these powerful technologies to improve their work.
[...]
- 3-4 years of work or educational experience in AI and Artificial Intelligence

- Experience with at least one general-purpose programming language including Java, C/C++, Python, or R

- Strong experience with Python for programming development and knowledge of complex methodologies and nuances of programming development in Python

- Experience with at least one of the following: Natural Language Processing, sentiment analysis, classification, or pattern recognition
[...]
- Development experience with AI frameworks such as TensorFlow, Microsoft CNTK, scikit-learn, Keras, Caffe, Gluon, LightGBM, SageMaker, or Azure AI
[...]
- Side projects you've worked on in the past couple of years in the AI/ML space

- Relevant experience in the travel, aviation, or airline industry

- Advanced degrees in Computer Science, Data Science, or related fields

- Experience with cloud platforms and distributed computing

- Knowledge of aircraft operations and logistics
[...]
you're
[...]
resume and a cover letter highlighting
[...]
- Any side projects you've worked on in the past couple of years in the AI/ML space

- Any relevant experience in the travel, aviation, or airline industry

- Your portfolio or examples of technical work
[...]
At arenaflex, we're looking for individuals who are excited about pushing the boundaries of what's possible in the aviation industry. If you're passionate about artificial intelligence, love solving complex problems, and want to make a real impact on how people travel, we want to hear from you.

# System Data Analyst – Flight Ops Support, DPS (PT)

Posted May 28, 2026

UNITED STATES, GEORGIA, ATLANTA

DELTA PROFESSIONAL SERVICES

18-MAR-2026

REF #: 31771

HOW YOU'LL HELP US KEEP CLIMBING (OVERVIEW & KEY RESPONSIBILITIES)

The part time Flights Ops Support role provides subject matter expert support to key projects and needs inside of Delta Professional Services (DPS). This role works on an ‘as needed’ basis supporting the business systems team with PowerBI and analysis. This position will report to the Business Systems Manager.

- ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS/TASKS:
- Analyze operational data and prepare weekly performance reports and visual summaries.
- Support Power BI development to enable data-driven decision-making.
- Collect, analyze, and interpret complex datasets to support business decision-making and operational efficiency.
- Ensure data integrity, automate routine reporting tasks, and support systems updates.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and fulfill data-related project tasks.
- Research and propose improvements for analytics tools, dashboards, and reporting processes.
- Document processes and assist with the development of data and reporting standards.

- BENEFITS AND PERKS TO HELP YOU KEEP CLIMBING
- Space Available Personal Travel to include eligible dependent
- Medical, Dental and Vision Plans available
- Profit Sharing
- 401(k) match (following one year of service)

- WHAT YOU NEED TO SUCCEED (MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS)
- Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data.
- Embraces a diverse set of people, thinking and styles.
- Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency.
- Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States.

- KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES:
- Proficiency or a good working knowledge in Tableau and/or data software systems
- Strong written, oral communication, and interpersonal skills
- Proficiency with Microsoft Excel and Power BI
- Must be able to work alone as well as in a team environment. Must be self-motivated and possess strong written and oral communication skills.
- Work in a fast-paced, newly established environment while maintaining an attention to detail

- COMPETENCY STATEMENTS:
- Analytical Skills - Ability to use thinking and reasoning to solve a problem.
- Problem Solving - Ability to make critical decisions while following company procedures.
- Attention to Detail – Ability to produce work that is compliant and error-free while meeting established deadlines.
- Diversity - Ability to work in a friendly, respectful, and professional manner with people regardless of their age, gender, race, ethnicity, religion, national origin, disability etc.
- Honesty/Integrity/Ethical Standards – Ability to maintain high moral standards and be honest and ethical while performing job duties.
- Innovative and strategic thinker.

WHAT WILL GIVE YOU A COMPETITIVE EDGE (PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS)

N/A

highlights:
Project Manager, Operations Strategy | Hirevector
[...]
# Project Manager, Operations Strategy
[...]
Posted Jun 1, 2026
[...]
At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all of our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience.
[...]
The Disruption Containment team at Delta improves the airline’s operations by promoting quicker, smarter decisions using real-time data, predictive analytics, collaboration, and analysis. The Disruption Containment strategy team in particular leads the strategic transformation of Delta’s Operations Control Center by designing and executing a forward-looking strategy to minimize operational disruptions, recover more quickly, and improve on Delta’s industry-leading reliability.
[...]
As an Operations Strategy project leader on this team, you will be responsible for analysis and insights development, but more importantly supporting design and implementation of a forward-looking strategy. You will serve as a cross-functional integrator, working most closely with the Director of Disruption Containment Strategy but also with other stakeholders across the Operation. We are looking for data curious individuals who seek to understand process, can connect with our business partners to leverage data, and ultimately identify opportunities and drive improvement. We are looking for candidates who enjoy thoughtful debates about how strategy, business process improvement, and analytics impact the way Delta reliably serves its customers.
[...]
The successful candidate will espouse Delta's core values of Honesty, Integrity, Respect, Perseverance, and Servant Leadership. Additionally, they will exhibit strategic vision, strong analytical/process skills, a bias for action, and the ability to partner with senior operational leaders to influence strategy, improve the Delta customer experience, improve operational reliability, and drive results.
[...]
- Think creatively about Operations process improvement opportunities based on data analysis and findings; drive and shepherd process improvement opportunities
[...]
- Understand drivers of KPIs through various analyses and work closely with the business team to create solutions
[...]
- Translate data into information, boiling down large amounts of data into actionable and tangible insights, focused on the “so what”
[...]
- Present findings to business leaders, including executives
[...]
- Build relationships and collaborate with teams across Delta’s Operation, including Operations Analytics, Crew Tracking, Crew Scheduling, Aircraft Routing, Tech Ops, Airport Customer Service and/or Reservations as needed
[...]
- What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in strategy or process improvement, leading to proficiency with numbers and analytics
- Strong critical thinking and ability to dissect a problem; broad base of business fundamentals
[...]
- Direct experience communicating with stakeholders, delivering projects end-to-end, and working collaboratively to drive improvement in the overall analytical support of Delta operations worldwide
[...]
- Proficiency with basic Microsoft tools including Excel and Powerpoint
[...]
- What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)
[...]
- Master’s degree in Business, or Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business/Management, Mathematics/Statistics, Economics, or other relevant quantitative field
- Experience in strategy consulting
- Previous strategy experience in complex operational roles. or other performance-based roles at an airline
- Understanding of airline operations, including crew management, aircraft routing, and customer recovery
[...]
- Familiarity and/or experience with some or all of the following: SQL, SAS, R, Tableau, PowerBI, Qlik, Spotfire or other database interface tools and languages

results.title: Analyst, Domestic Revenue Management | Hirevector
highlights:
Analyst, Domestic Revenue Management | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# Analyst, Domestic Revenue Management

Posted May 30, 2026

Become a member of Delta’s World Class Domestic Pricing and Revenue Management team as a Domestic Revenue Management Analyst. Delta’s RM professionals are responsible for maximizing the revenue generated on each flight and customer segment by aligning passenger demand with willingness to pay to offer the optimal mix of fare products. Core to Delta’s industry-leading revenue results, each Analyst uses a rigorous, innovative set of demand management and competitive pricing strategies. Every Revenue Management Analyst blends the art of demand management with the science of analytical expertise to create the right price for each customer on every flight.

As part of the department onboarding process, you will participate in a training cohort with the expectation of successful completion of the training courses and knowledge assessments.

- DOM PRM Team Functions:
- If placed in a Pricing role, you will monitor, initiate and communicate industry pricing actions including changes to fare levels, fare rules, policies, and pricing strategy execution
- Proactively manage Delta’s fare structure including prices and fare rules
- Identify and analyze market issues and trends, determining appropriate pricing actions, filing of actions, and post-audit assessment of actions
- If placed in an Inventory management role, you will be responsible for flight level revenue optimization by properly valuing our passengers and forecasting future demand levels
- Monitor and update the flight level demand forecasts through the analysis of historical and future booking trends, the pricing environment, industry capacity trends, the competitive landscape, and other factors
- Establish and maintain the flight level overbooking forecasts through the analysis of no-show behavior trends
- Use various and complex high-level dashboards, prescriptive to descriptive analysis, along with market specific data, to create an appropriate customer offer to best segment customers and maximize revenue
- Diagnose, interpret, and communicate basic market performance of revenue stats: LF, Yield, RASM, local vs. flow, drivers and actions
- Evaluate performance and post-departure trends to identify and leverage future opportunities while maximizing the revenue potential for each market
- Performing frequent ad-hoc analyses related to the assigned markets
- Communicate market trends, updates, recommendations and overall performance results to Revenue Management leaders and key stakeholders within the Commercial team
- Provide technical support to other revenue management groups and both internal and external distribution channels and reporting
- Work cross-functionally with Inventory Management, Pricing, Network Planning, Alliances, Sales, Marketing, and other teams to ensure alignment on market level strategies

Apply tot his job

# Associate CASS Data Scientist
[...]
Posted Jun 6, 2026
[...]
As an Associate CASS Data Scientist, you’ll help drive the analytical backbone of Southwest’s Continuing Analysis and Surveillance System (CASS), turning complex operational data into insights that strengthen safety, compliance, and reliability. You’ll develop automated workflows, build models, and apply advanced analytics—including machine learning and AI—to detect trends, assess risk, and optimize the health of our Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program. Partnering closely with Tech Ops, CASS Leadership, and cross‑functional stakeholders, you’ll create dashboards and predictive tools that inform critical decisions and support the ongoing improvement of our maintenance programs. By blending strong analytical methods with real‑world operational understanding, you’ll play a key role in ensuring our fleet stays safe, efficient, and ready to serve our Customers.

[...]
# Applied Data Scientist
[...]
Posted Jun 8, 2026
[...]
Your Role at Delta Faucet
[...]
Are you passionate about using data science, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics to propel decisions? If you are interested in driving meaningful change for a growth focused consumer products brand, this is an opportunity to join the market-leading Delta Faucet Company team as we use analytics to turbocharge strategic insights!
[...]
Reporting to the Manager of Data Science and Analytics, this role will work with analytics and domain specialists across the business to deliver high-impact insights and data science solutions. You will develop advanced analytical models, experimentation tests, applied AI techniques, and partner with business stakeholders to translate complex data into clear, actionable recommendations that support the company’s growth initiatives.
[...]
As an Applied Data Scientist within Delta Faucet Company’s Analytics Center of Excellence (ACoE), you will act as an owner of high impact analysis and data science initiatives that support complex, ambiguous business decisions. This role focuses on advanced analytics, experimentation, and applied AI.
[...]
- Independently lead end-to-end analytics and data science projects, from problem framing through delivery of actionable insights.
- Apply statistical methods, machine learning, and analytical modeling to solve business problems related to growth, customer behavior, market dynamics, and operational efficiency.
- Develop and maintain analytical data models and curated datasets, partnering with data engineering and BI teams.
- Build AI and machine learning solutions that are aligned with business needs.
- Collaborate closely with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into clear recommendations.
- Present data-driven recommendations to leaders across the organization, including tactical and C-suite.
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in data science, advanced analytics, or applied machine learning roles.
- 4+ years of hands-on experience applying statistical methods and analytical modeling to real business problems.
- 4+ years of experience using Python for data analysis, modeling, and experimentation.
- 4+ years of experience writing and refining complex SQL queries against large, structured datasets.
- 3+ years of experience partnering directly with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into actionable recommendations.
- 2+ years of experience working in a modern analytics platform (e.g., Databricks, Spark/PySpark, or similar).
- 1+ years of hands-on‑ experience applying generative AI technologies (e.g., LLM APIs, task‑specific GenAI workflows) in an enterprise analytics environment.
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Data Science, Economics, or a related quantitative field.
[...]
- 2+ years of experience with analytics engineering and data modeling, including building reusable, well-documented analytical datasets; familiarity with medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) preferred.
- 1+ years of experience with model monitoring, experiment tracking, and model evaluation in production settings.
- 1+ years of experience with conjoint analysis or other stated-preference research methods.

Company: Delta Faucet Company

# Data Scientist

Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.

Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. preferably in information technology,

At GE Aerospace, we invent the future of flight, lift people up, and bring them home safely. The AI Forward Deployed Engineering (AI FDE) team is a highly specialized customer-facing AI team at Databricks. Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. results.title: Inside ATL: how Delta juggles 100,000 bags a day at the world's busiest airport | NPR & Houston Public Media
highlights:
"Atlanta is an enormous operation, Delta's biggest by a long way," said Paul Buckley, Delta's director of operations here. To manage all of this complexity, the airline built its own AI system to help its tug drivers move bags more efficiently, like a ridesharing algorithm.
[...]
The AI system helps drivers prioritize which bags need to go first.
[...]
have to focus
[...]
But the AI system is not perfect, Davis said. Sometimes it's given him some very tight connections.
[...]
Delta Air Lines ramp agent Mike Davis uses a handheld electronic scanner with Delta's Radio Frequency Identification and Artificial Intelligence technology to locate passenger luggage after unloading from a plane at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport. (Alyssa Pointer for NPR)
[...]
Delta says the new AI system has improved its baggage transfer success rates by as much as 20%. The airline says it plans to expand the system to its other hubs in Detroit and Minneapolis-Saint Paul later this year.
[...]
Still, Delta says AI will not displace its human ramp employees.
[...]
"We don't see AI as something that is going to replace our people," Buckley said. "We see AI as an enabler, an enabler of performance, and giving the tools to our people to go produce at an even better level."
[...]
The system has been especially helpful for newer drivers, Delta managers said. And it's making the job easier for veterans like Mike Davis, who's won awards for being one of Delta's best tug drivers.
[...]
"You know how they say, 'out with the old and in with the new?' That's what it is. It's called continuous improvement," he said. "You have to change with the times to be relevant."
[...]
ROSE: Paul Buckley is Delta's director of operations in Atlanta. To manage all this complexity, Buckley says the airline built its own AI system to help its tug drivers move bags more efficiently, like a ride-sharing app.
[...]
ROSE: The new AI system helps drivers prioritize which bags need to go first.
[...]
ROSE: Delta managers say they don't see this AI system as a replacement for their ramp employees but as an enhancer to make them more efficient. Delta says it's been especially helpful for newer drivers. And it's making the job easier for veterans like Mike Davis, who's won awards for being one of Delta's best tug drivers.
[...]
ROSE: Delta says the new system has boosted efficiency by as much as 20% while helping cut the number of missed bags. That's the kind of improvement travelers notice, even if they never see where it happens.

Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow.
